creator: Ivan Mehta
title: (No AI) After Europe, WhatsApp will let rival AI companies offer chatbots in Brazil
dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
content: Meta is now allowing rival AI companies to provide their chatbots on WhatsApp to Brazilian users for a fee, a day after the company confirmed a similar decision for users in Europe.
contentSnippet: Meta is now allowing rival AI companies to provide their chatbots on WhatsApp to Brazilian users for a fee, a day after the company confirmed a similar decision for users in Europe.

creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) Anthropic to challenge DOD’s supply-chain label in court
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei said he plans to challenge the Department of Defense's designation of the AI firm as a supply-chain risk. He claims most Anthropic customers are unaffected by the label.
contentSnippet: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei said he plans to challenge the Department of Defense's designation of the AI firm as a supply-chain risk. He claims most Anthropic customers are unaffected by the label.

title: (No AI) The Pentagon formally labels Anthropic a supply-chain risk
author: Tina Nguyen
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/gettyimages-2263863571.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth speaks during a press conference on US military action in Iran, at the Pentagon in Washington, DC, on March 2, 2026. | Brendan Smialowski/AFP via Getty Images

After weeks of [failed negotiations, public ultimatums, and lawsuit threats](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/886082/ai-vs-the-pentagon-killer-robots-mass-surveillance-and-red-lines), the Defense Department has formally labeled Anthropic a "supply-chain risk," escalating its fight with the AI company over their acceptable use policies and potentially bringing their fight to court.

The decision, [first reported by _The Wall Street Journal_ on Thursday](https://www.wsj.com/politics/national-security/pentagon-formally-labels-anthropic-supply-chain-risk-escalating-conflict-ebdf0523?mod=rss_Technology), citing one source familiar, will bar defense contractors from working with the government if they use Claude, Anthropic's AI program, in their products. Though the designation is typically applied to foreign companies with ties to adversarial governments, this is the first time that an American company …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/890347/pentagon-anthropic-supply-chain-risk)
contentSnippet:
US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth speaks during a press conference on US military action in Iran, at the Pentagon in Washington, DC, on March 2, 2026. | Brendan Smialowski/AFP via Getty Images	

After weeks of failed negotiations, public ultimatums, and lawsuit threats, the Defense Department has formally labeled Anthropic a "supply-chain risk," escalating its fight with the AI company over their acceptable use policies and potentially bringing their fight to court.
The decision, first reported by The Wall Street Journal on Thursday, citing one source familiar, will bar defense contractors from working with the government if they use Claude, Anthropic's AI program, in their products. Though the designation is typically applied to foreign companies with ties to adversarial governments, this is the first time that an American company …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: After weeks of failed negotiations, public ultimatums, and lawsuit threats, the Defense Department has formally labeled Anthropic a "supply-chain risk," escalating its fight with the AI company over their acceptable use policies and potentially bringing their fight to court. The decision, first reported by The Wall Street Journal on Thursday, citing one source familiar, will […]

title: (No AI) Netflix is buying Ben Affleck’s AI startup
author: Charles Pulliam-Moore
content:
![Actor Ben Affleck in a suit.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/gettyimages-2256155551.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Though [Netflix lost the war for Warner Bros.](https://www.theverge.com/streaming/885753/netflix-exit-warner-bros-discovery-deal-paramount), it has just bought an AI startup from [the internet's favorite Dunkin' Donuts aficionado](https://www.theverge.com/news/864107/ben-affleck-says-smart-things-about-ai).

Today, Netflix announced that it has acquired InterPositive, Ben Affleck's AI company that specializes in tools for film and television production. The deal will see all 16 of InterPositive's current team of engineers and researchers move over to Netflix. Affleck is also set to join the streamer as a senior adviser.

In [a statement about the acquisition](https://about.netflix.com/en/news/why-interpositive-is-joining-netflix) and his reasons for founding InterPositive in 2022, Affleck said he was inspired to get into the tech space after "observing the early rise of AI in producti …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/streaming/889973/netflix-ben-affleck-interpositive-ai)

The deal will see all 16 of InterPositive's current team of engineers and researchers move over to Netflix. Affleck is also set to join the streamer as a senior adviser.
In a statement about the acquisition and his reasons for founding InterPositive in 2022, Affleck said he was inspired to get into the tech space after "observing the early rise of AI in producti …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Reasoning models struggle to control their chains of thought, and that’s good

creator: Reece Rogers
title: (No AI) Why Is Alexa+ So Bad?
dc:creator: Reece Rogers
content: I stuck Amazon’s Echo Show 15 and its Alexa+ AI assistant in my kitchen for a month. Things have not gone well.
contentSnippet: I stuck Amazon’s Echo Show 15 and its Alexa+ AI assistant in my kitchen for a month. Things have not gone well.

creator: Steven Levy
title: (No AI) Jack Dorsey Is Ready to Explain the Block Layoffs
dc:creator: Steven Levy
content: In an exclusive interview with WIRED, Block’s cofounder and CEO says he axed 40 percent of his workforce so that he can rebuild the company “as an intelligence.”
contentSnippet: In an exclusive interview with WIRED, Block’s cofounder and CEO says he axed 40 percent of his workforce so that he can rebuild the company “as an intelligence.”
